mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1,100,000 signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1100000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 0.045 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 106, indicating a strengthening manufacturing sector, signals a positive supply shock that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 106 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4.49 trillion signals a strengthening consumer sector that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 4490000000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 143,288,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 143288000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 238.847 signals a strengthening inflationary pressure that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 238.847 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 16,442.3 billion signals a strengthening economic growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 1644230000000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
